title: Assessment of Clinical Criteria for Sepsis For the Third International Consensus Definitions for Sepsis and Septic Shock (Sepsis-3)
country: United States (primary cohort); also includes Germany and other non-US sites

sample_size.source_quote: In the primary cohort, 148 907 encounters had suspected infection (n = 74 453 derivation; n = 74 454 validation), of whom 6347 (4%) died.

sofa_score_baseline.source_quote: SOFA near onset of suspected infection Mean (SD) 2.0 (2.7)

icu_mortality_percent.source_quote: Among ICU encounters in the validation cohort (n = 7932 with suspected infection, of whom 1289 [16%] died)

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, race/ethnicity, weighted Charlson comorbidity score
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Among ICU encounters in the validation cohort (n = 7932 with suspected infection, of whom 1289 [16%] died), the predictive validity for in-hospital mortality was lower for SIRS (AUROC = 0.64; 95% CI, 0.62-0.66) and qSOFA (AUROC = 0.66; 95% CI, 0.64-0.68) vs SOFA (AUROC = 0.74; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76; P < .001 for both)

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, race/ethnicity, weighted Charlson comorbidity score
mortality_predictors.source_quote: or LODS (AUROC = 0.75; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76; P < .001 for both)

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, race/ethnicity, weighted Charlson comorbidity score
mortality_predictors.source_quote: the predictive validity for in-hospital mortality was lower for SIRS (AUROC = 0.64; 95% CI, 0.62-0.66)

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, race/ethnicity, weighted Charlson comorbidity score
mortality_predictors.source_quote: qSOFA (AUROC = 0.66; 95% CI, 0.64-0.68) vs SOFA (AUROC = 0.74; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76; P < .001 for both)

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.effect_size: 3- to 14-fold increase in hospital mortality for qSOFA ≥2 vs <2
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, race/ethnicity, weighted Charlson comorbidity score
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Among non-ICU encounters in the validation cohort (n = 66 522 with suspected infection, of whom 1886 [3%] died), qSOFA had predictive validity (AUROC = 0.81; 95% CI, 0.80-0.82) that was greater than SOFA (AUROC = 0.79; 95% CI, 0.78-0.80; P < .001) and SIRS (AUROC = 0.76; 95% CI, 0.75-0.77; P < .001). Relative to qSOFA scores lower than 2, encounters with qSOFA scores of 2 or higher had a 3- to 14-fold increase in hospital mortality across baseline risk deciles.

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, race/ethnicity, weighted Charlson comorbidity score
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The discrimination of hospital mortality using SOFA (AUROC = 0.79; 95% CI, 0.78-0.80), LODS (AUROC = 0.82; 95% CI, 0.81-0.83), or change in SOFA (AUROC = 0.79; 95% CI, 0.78-0.79) scores was significantly greater compared with SIRS criteria (AUROC = 0.76; 95% CI, 0.75-0.77; P < .01 for all)

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, race/ethnicity, Charlson comorbidity index, respiratory rate, systolic blood pressure
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Altered mental status, Glasgow Coma Scale score 14-15 66 879 1677 (3) 1 [Reference] ≤13 7574 1495 (20) 4.31 (3.96-4.69)

Age, sex, race/ethnicity, Charlson comorbidity index, respiratory rate, GCS score

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.specificity: 10% of survivors had SOFA <2 (i.e., 90% specificity for SOFA ≥2)
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The SOFA score was 2 or more in 98% of decedents (95% CI, 97%-99%); among survivors, the SOFA score was less than 2 in 10% (95% CI, 10%-11%).

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Seventy percent (95% CI, 69%-72%) of decedents had 2 or more qSOFA points and 78% (95% CI, 78%-79%) of survivors had less than 2 qSOFA points
